hypothesis: Incorporating binary substructure-concept predictors (for a designated scaffold and key functional groups) into CAST’s concept bottleneck and learning corresponding orthogonal steering embeddings will enable the steered LLM to retain the specified scaffold in ≥90% of generated molecules while matching target QED and LogP with MAEs within 0.05, outperforming standard CAST (without substructure concepts) which will retain the scaffold in <50% of cases and exhibit larger MAE deviations.
